_about: Canonical cross-engine vectors for the N-way playlist 3-way merge. BOTH the Kotlin (mobile, shared/.../sync/PlaylistMerge.kt) and the JS (desktop, app.js) merge implementations MUST produce `expected` for each case, exactly. This file is the contract that keeps the two engines from drifting and fighting over the same remote playlists. Mobile's PlaylistMergeTest is a 1:1 transcription of these cases; desktop transcribes the same cases into its JS test.

_semantics.presence: union-adds: a key in ANY changed copy but not in the baseline is added. union-removes: a baseline key missing from ANY changed copy is removed. present = (baseline - removed) ∪ added. DELETE ALWAYS WINS — a baseline key dropped by any copy is gone even if a newer copy still has it (a copy still holding the key is un-propagated baseline, NOT a re-add). `added` and `removed` are disjoint by construction (added keys are non-baseline; removed keys are baseline), so there is no same-key add-vs-delete race to tiebreak.
_semantics.timestamps: editedAt affects ORDER ONLY, never presence.
_semantics.order: Walk the most-recently-edited changed copy's tracks first, then the baseline, then each other changed copy in input order; keep only present keys; dedupe (first occurrence wins). A copy whose tracks == baseline contributes no delta and is ignored.
_semantics.key_derivation: Canonical track key precedence: validateIsrc(isrc) -> 'isrc-<UPPERCASED, regex ^[A-Z]{2}[A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]{7}$>'; else recordingMbid -> 'mbid-<trim+lowercase>'; else 'norm-<artist trim+lowercase>|<title trim+lowercase>'. The norm fallback is lower+trim ONLY — NOT the strip-non-alphanumeric normalize used in confidence scoring.

cases.name: delete_wins_even_if_newer_copy_still_has_key
cases._note: THE divergence case. Older copy deleted x; newer copy still has x (un-propagated baseline, not a re-add). x is DROPPED. editedAt does not resurrect a deleted key.
